sentence: Subjective:
HPI: The patient, a 54-year-old male, presents with a 2-3 month history of stabbing, shooting pain in the right cheekbone area, which is exacerbated by cold temperatures and wind.
The pain is described as excruciating and lasts from a few minutes to an hour.
The patient reports that he has tried ibuprofen, Motrin, and Tiger Balm without relief.
He has had occasional headaches but no history of migraines or brain tumors.
He rates the pain as a 10 on a scale of 1-10.
Past medical history: The patient has not mentioned any past medical conditions, treatments, or encounters.
He denies any history of multiple sclerosis or brain tumors.
Review of systems: The patient reports no sensory loss, numbness, or tingling in the affected area.
He denies weakness on the right side of his face and reports that his smile seems equal on both sides.
He has no vision changes.
Current medications: None mentioned.
Objective:
Vital signs: Not mentioned.
Physical exam: The patient's smile appears symmetric, and he can hear equally well on both sides of his face.
The patient reports a little bit of numbness on the right side of his face when cotton tape is run along the side of his face.
When the doctor pokes the area with a needle, the patient reports a dull sensation on the right side and a sharp sensation on the left side.
Test Results: The patient's MRI shows a small blood vessel abutting and pinching the trigeminal nerve.
Assessment / Problem List:
Assessment: The patient has symptoms consistent with trigeminal neuralgia, a condition where nerve compression causes shooting electrical pain in the face.
Problem list: Trigeminal neuralgia; active.
Plan:
I prescribed Tegretol 100mg twice a day for the patient's trigeminal neuralgia.
I will schedule a follow-up appointment in one month to check on the patient's response to the medication.
The patient will have genetic testing to ensure the medication is safe for them.
Follow-up: The patient will return in one month to check on the medication's effectiveness and discuss further treatment options if necessary.
